shipped_headline: Smart routing: get the right ticket to the right agent
post_mortem: Buried the customer payoff in paragraph 4. Long technical preamble up top. CTR ~30% lower than auto-summary and sentiment-tag. Lesson: lead with the customer story, not the architecture.

shipped_headline: Powerful new summarization for your support queue
post_mortem: First headline used adjectives (powerful) and underperformed the eventual rewrite by 2x. Body was good — customer story in the lead, one quantified time-saved stat, beta quote in the middle. The headline was the weak link.

shipped_headline: Your angriest tickets, surfaced before they escalate
post_mortem: Best performer of the three. Concrete-moment headline, customer story lead, quantified outcome anchored to escalations avoided (time), beta-customer quote in paragraph 3 of 5, closed with a question. This is the structure to copy.
Decent. Quantified outcome in the headline worked. Closed with a CTA (call-to-action) rather than a question and the close felt flat — questions outperformed CTAs in A/B.
Contrast headline (what you stop doing) landed well. Anchored on time saved per week. Lesson reinforced: anchor on time, not feature counts.
